person.name: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlerton

sibling.name: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlerton

km.Strafforth and Tickhill, Yorkshire, Engl: stរាfប៉ូរត អាន ធីកកហ៊ីល, យ៉ុរកស៊ីរe, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.Strafforth and Tickhill, Yorkshire, Engl: 英國越可爾斯特拉福斯 安德 迪可爾
zh.lifeSummary: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlerton是FitzWulfstan de Sheffield的兒子，於大約1100年出生在英國越可爾斯特拉福斯 安德 迪可爾。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.Strafforth and Tickhill, Yorkshire, Engl: 英国越可爾斯特拉福斯 安德 迪可爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlerton是FitzWulfstan de Sheffield的儿子，于大约1100年出生在英国越可爾斯特拉福斯 安德 迪可爾。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sheffield,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Sheffield (Yorkshire), but perhaps also from Sheffield Bottom in Burghfield (Berkshire), or Sheffield Park in Fletching (Sussex). The Yorkshire place takes its name from the river Sheaf, itself from Old English scēath ‘boundary’, + Old English feld ‘open country’, while the Berkshire placename derives from Old English scēo ‘shelter’ + feld. The Sussex placename comes from Old English scēap ‘sheep’ + feld.

variants.name: Shuffield
meanings: English: variant of   Sheffield  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Wadsley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Wadsley in Sheffield (Yorkshire), which is recorded as Wadesley and Waddesley in medieval documents. The placename derives from an Old English personal name, Wæddi (genitive Wæddes) + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ali, Hossein, Abbas, Behnaz, Gitti, Hamid, Jafar, Mansour, Masoud, Mehdi, Pari, Rana.  Jewish Jechiel, Bracha, Herschel, Morry.
Iranian: from Zand, the name of a dinasty which ruled Persia 1751–94. It is based on Middle Persian zand ‘great, powerful’ and was originally the name of a former Iranian tribe.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Sand  .

variants.name: Sand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Bertel, Ejnar, Lars, Levinus, Nels, Niels, Thor.

English, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived on or near a patch of sandy soil, from the vocabulary word sand (German Sand). As a Swedish name it was often purely ornamental, and as a Jewish artificial. It is also found in France (see 3 below).
English: habitational name from a place such as Send (Surrey) or Sound (Cheshire), named with Middle English sand, sond ‘sand’.

variants.name: Zant
meanings: German: variant of   Zand  .
Dutch: variant of   Van Zandt  .

variants.name: Zandi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mahmud, Taher, Ahmad, Azi, Bahram, Behzad, Farman, Firooz, Hamid, Hossein, Jaleh, Masood.
Iranian: derivative of   Zand  , denoting descent from or association with a Persian dinasty of that name.
Italian (Emilia): perhaps a nickname from the dialect word gianda ‘acorn’.

nameMeanings.name: Owlerton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Ollerton in Lancashire. The placename derives from Old English alor ‘alder’ + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’. There are similarly named places in Cheshire, Nottinghamshire, and Yorkshire. Compare Allerton .

variants.name: Allerton
meanings: English:

habitational name from any of several places so called. Allerton on Merseyside, Chapel Allerton in Yorkshire, and others in Yorkshire were named in Old English as alra tūn ‘settlement by the alders’. One in Somerset (Alwarditone in Domesday Book) is ‘Ælfweard's settlement’; one in Yorkshire (Allerton Mauleverer, Alvertone in Domesday Book) is ‘Ælfhere's settlement’.
(Suffolk): probably a variant of   Alderton  .

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlerton was born about 1100, in Strafforth and Tickhill, Yorkshire, England as the son of FitzWulfstan de Sheffield. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlerton est né environ 1100, à Strafforth and Tickhill, Everwyck, Angleterre, fils de FitzWulfstan de Sheffield. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlerton wurde um 1100 in Strafforth and Tickhill, Yorkshire, England, als Sohn von FitzWulfstan de Sheffield geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlerton è nato il circa 1100 nel Strafforth and Tickhill, Yorkshire, England come figlio di FitzWulfstan de Sheffield. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Strafforth and Tickhill, Yorkshire, Engl: イギリスヨークシュアイアストラフォース アンド ティックヒル
ja.lifeSummary: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlertonは約1100年にイギリスヨークシュアイアストラフォース アンド ティックヒルでFitzWulfstan de Sheffieldの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlerton(은)는 대략 1100년 영국 요르크실르 스트래포스 앤드 티크힐에서 FitzWulfstan de Sheffield의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlerton nasceu aproximadamente 1100, em Strafforth and Tickhill, Yorkshire, Inglaterra filho de FitzWulfstan de Sheffield. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlerton родился около 1100 г. в местности страффорт анд тиккхилл, еркшир, Англия. Его отца звали FitzWulfstan de Sheffield. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Baron de Sheffield, Wadsley and Owlerton nació aproximadamente 1100, en Strafforth and Tickhill, Yorkshire, Inglaterra como hijo de FitzWulfstan de Sheffield. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
